name: Validic Remote Patient Monitoring Data Platform API
what_it_does: Validic remote patient monitoring data platform REST API for health systems, payers, and digital health companies to collect and normalize health data from wearables, medical devices, and patient-reported outcomes. Enables AI agents to manage device and app connection for patient health data collection automation, handle multi-source data normalization and standardization for health analytics integration automation, access real-time biometric data streaming for remote patient monitoring automation, retrieve chronic disease monitoring data for care management program automation, manage alert and threshold configuration for clinical intervention automation, handle data quality validation and outlier detection for clinical data reliability automation, access population health cohort analytics for remote monitoring program performance automation, retrieve FHIR-compliant health data export for EHR integration automation, manage patient enrollment and device provisioning for RPM program onboarding automation, and integrate Validic with Epic, Cerner, and major EHR systems for end-to-end remote patient monitoring program management.
use_cases: Collecting wearable data from RPM program automation agents
Monitoring chronic disease from care management automation agents
Triggering clinical alerts from remote monitoring intervention agents
Reporting RPM outcomes from digital health analytics agents
not_for: Clinical-grade diagnostic device data (FDA Class II/III medical devices)
Research-grade biometric data collection (use Axivity or ActiGraph)
best_when: A health system, payer, or employer wellness program wanting AI agents to automate patient-generated health data collection from consumer wearables and medical devices, biometric threshold alerting, and chronic disease monitoring within Validic's unified RPM data platform.
avoid_when: FDA SOFTWARE AS MEDICAL DEVICE CLASSIFICATION: Automated clinical decision support triggered by Validic device data may constitute Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) requiring FDA clearance; automated clinical alerts that recommend specific treatment or medication changes based on Validic biometric data without FDA clearance creates regulatory exposure. HIPAA PHI FOR DEVICE DATA: Automated collection of health device data linked to patient identity via Validic constitutes PHI; Validic operates as HIPAA Business Associate; automated integration must maintain BAA and HIPAA-compliant data handling for all linked biometric data. CMS RPM BILLING REQUIREMENT FOR 20-MINUTE MONITORING: Medicare RPM billing (CPT 99457/99458) requires at least 20 minutes of interactive monitoring per month; automated RPM data collection via Validic supports billing documentation but automated billing without verifying 20-minute threshold creates Medicare billing compliance issue.

auth.notes: Validic uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes. REST API with JSON and mobile SDKs. Durham, North Carolina HQ. Founded 2010 by Drew Schiller and Ryan Ribeira. Private (~$30M raised). Health data platform for RPM and consumer health data. 500+ device and app integrations (Fitbit, Apple Health, Garmin, Omron, iHealth). FHIR data output. Epic and Cerner integration. HIPAA Business Associate. Competes with Dario Health, Apple Health, and Google Health API for health data aggregation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-patient per-month subscription for RPM programs
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-patient monthly subscription; enterprise pricing for large health system RPM programs; API access included in subscription
pricing.notes: Durham NC. Private. Founded 2010. Per-patient monthly subscription. Health system RPM program pricing. FHIR output. No free tier.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; health data and patient queries throttled; webhooks for device data and alert events

known_agent_gotchas: DEVICE DATA LATENCY VARIES BY DEVICE SYNC MODEL: Validic device data latency varies significantly by device sync mechanism; Bluetooth-paired devices have near-real-time sync while cloud-polled devices (Fitbit, Garmin) have hourly or daily sync lag; automated alert thresholds configured for real-time response may have delayed trigger for cloud-polled devices; design clinical alert workflows with device-specific sync latency tolerance

CMS RPM BILLING 20-MINUTE THRESHOLD DOCUMENTATION: Automated RPM billing documentation via Validic must track cumulative monitoring time to meet CMS 99457 minimum 20-minute interactive monitoring per month; automated time tracking must distinguish synchronous interactive monitoring from passive data collection; automated billing without confirmed 20-minute threshold creates Medicare claim with insufficient documentation

PATIENT-DEVICE PAIRING AUTHORIZATION SCOPE: Validic patient-device connection requires explicit patient authorization for data sharing; automated device provisioning must implement Validic patient connection workflow with explicit consent; automated data collection before patient authorization creates HIPAA unauthorized PHI collection

DATA NORMALIZATION CROSS-DEVICE CALIBRATION: Validic normalizes device data to common units but source device calibration varies; automated analytics that compare biometric values across different device manufacturers must account for device-specific measurement methodology; automated cross-device comparison without calibration acknowledgment creates clinical accuracy misrepresentation

DEVICE ECOSYSTEM PARTNER DEPENDENCY: Validic relies on third-party device manufacturer APIs (Fitbit, Garmin, Apple, Omron); upstream device API changes by device manufacturers may affect Validic data collection; automated RPM workflows should monitor Validic device partner status for connectivity issues from upstream API changes

FHIR OUTPUT FORMAT COMPLIANCE VERSION: Validic FHIR output supports FHIR R4 and earlier versions; automated EHR integration must specify compatible FHIR version; FHIR version mismatch between Validic output and EHR FHIR server creates data ingestion failure for automated RPM data integration
agent_readiness.security_notes: Remote patient monitoring. HIPAA, SOC2. OAuth2. US. Patient biometric data, health device data, and clinical monitoring records.

security_score_components.security_notes: RPM platform. HIPAA, SOC2. OAuth2. US. Patient biometric, health device, and clinical monitoring data.
